metadata.title: Why the Rb Gate Has Six Different Keys — and Losing Any One Opens It
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Biology candidate 10. Source chapter: cancer-biology/chapters/12-cell-cycle-control-and-cancer-disruption-and-therapy.md (Rb/E2F convergence and BRAF V600E melanoma case only — no resistance mechanisms, no breast cancer CDK4/6 trial data).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per graphic at most
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, teal = intact gate / controlled / arrested, crimson = open gate / lost brake / dividing uncontrolled

metadata.color_semantics: teal #1F6F5C = the gate held / brake applied / cell in check; crimson #BF3339 = gate forced open / brake lost / S-phase despite no signal; gold = editor's-pen highlight fill (once per graphic, never text); never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO BRAF inhibitor resistance mechanisms, NO full MAPK pathway details, NO PI3K pathway beyond cyclin D1 stabilization via AKT, NO cyclin E amplification as resistance route, NO breast cancer CDK4/6 inhibitor trial details. BRAF V600E melanoma with three converging alterations is the illustrative case; all pathway-level numbers and cell responses labeled illustrative in FACTCHECK. Manim move: accumulate.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A melanoma cell keeps dividing. The driver mutation is blocked. Division continues. A second pathway is blocked. Still dividing. The driver is not the limiting factor.

card.copy: Why the Rb gate has six different keys — and losing any one opens it

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] eyebrow CANCER BIOLOGY teal, title in ink

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] eyebrow CANCER BIOLOGY teal, title in ink

beats.narration_text: BRAF V600E drives roughly half of cutaneous melanomas. Vemurafenib, a BRAF inhibitor, produces dramatic tumor shrinkage within weeks. Scans show lesions clearing.

props.headline: BRAF V600E is the mutation driving half of all cutaneous melanomas.…

beats.scene_description: A PET scan image showing a patient's torso with melanoma metastases as bright spots; desaturated newsprint treatment; slow push-in toward the lesions
beats.image_prompt: B02, PET scan axial cross-section of a human torso showing bright fluorescent metastatic melanoma lesions in lymph nodes and soft tissue, medical imaging printed on aged newsprint, desaturated flat editorial collage treatment, no text overlay
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] PET scan showing melanoma metastases — dramatic initial response

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] PET scan showing melanoma metastases — dramatic initial response

beats.narration_text: The tumor returns. BRAF is still blocked; the drug still hits its target. Division continues anyway. BRAF inhibition should shut down the proliferative signal in a BRAF-mutant melanoma. Why does blocking the driver fail to stop division?

card.copy: BRAF inhibition should shut down the proliferative signal in a BRAF-mutant melanoma. Here BRAF is blocked, yet the cancer continues dividing. Why?

beats.new_visual_element: [QUESTION CARD] the film's question on screen

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [QUESTION CARD] the film's question on screen

beats.narration_text: Multiple upstream signals converge on a single gate. Blocking one signal does not close a gate that other signals are still opening. To understand why, you need to know what the signals are actually controlling.
At the G1/S boundary sits one protein: Rb. Active Rb binds E2F — the transcription factor that initiates DNA replication. E2F held means no S phase. This is the gate.

production_viz.label: Rb holds E2F — gate closed
production_viz.mechanic: A gate symbol in the center: a teal Rb rectangle gripping an E2F circle; label 'G1' on the left side, 'S phase' on the right with a barrier; serifs label each component as they appear

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Rb grips E2F — the gate in its closed, healthy state

beats.narration_text: Growth signals phosphorylate Rb through CDK4 and CDK6, each requiring a cyclin D partner. Phosphorylated Rb releases E2F. E2F drives S-phase gene transcription. Normal gate opening — conditional on signal.

production_viz.label: growth signal -> cyclin D-CDK4/6 -> Rb phosphorylated -> E2F free
production_viz.mechanic: A single input arrow 'growth signal' arrives from above; cyclin D-CDK4/6 chip appears; a phosphorylation mark tags Rb; Rb releases E2F; E2F moves right toward S phase; the gate swings open

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] normal opening: one signal, kinase pair, Rb released

beats.narration_text: In normal cells, gate opening is conditional on growth signal. In cancer, six distinct molecular lesions each force the same outcome: Rb phosphorylated, E2F free, S phase entered without a signal.

Rb deleted: gate gone, E2F free from the start. p16 lost: CDK4/CDK6 run unbraked, phosphorylating Rb continuously. Cyclin D amplified: kinase complex overwhelms residual p16. CDK4 or CDK6 amplified. Activating CDK4 mutation that blocks p16 binding. Hyperactive upstream signaling — BRAF, KRAS, PI3K — driving cyclin D continuously. Six routes. One endpoint.

production_viz.label: six lesions accumulate into the same E2F-free endpoint
production_viz.mechanic: Six crimson LabelChips accumulate one at a time on the left column — Rb loss, p16 loss, Cyclin D amplification, CDK4/6 amplification, CDK4 activating mutation, Hyperactive upstream signaling — each with a short arrow pointing right toward a single crimson endpoint chip 'E2F free / S phase'. The accumulate motion: each chip fades in and its arrow draws on sequentially.
production_viz.note: all six in crimson; convergence is the visual point

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] six crimson lesions accumulate toward one crimson endpoint

beats.narration_text: This convergence is selection pressure, not coincidence. Any cell that loses conditional control at this gate gains a replication advantage. Tumor evolution finds every molecular path to that outcome.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [SECTION CARD] Three Inputs One Melanoma

beats.narration_text: BRAF V600E melanomas typically carry three simultaneous alterations at the Rb gate. First: BRAF V600E activates MAPK, driving cyclin D1 transcription continuously. Second: CDKN2A/p16 is deleted in 50–70% of melanomas — CDK4/6 run without the brake. Third: PTEN loss activates PI3K and AKT, stabilizing cyclin D1 protein through a separate pathway.

production_viz.label: three alterations accumulate onto the CDK4/6 junction
production_viz.mechanic: A central junction box labeled CDK4/6 -> Rb sits at center-right. Three input cables arrive from the left, appearing one at a time (accumulate): cable 1 'BRAF V600E -> MAPK -> cyclin D1' in crimson; cable 2 'p16 loss -> CDK4/6 uninhibited' in crimson; cable 3 'PTEN loss -> AKT -> cyclin D1 stable' in crimson. Each cable plugs into the junction box as it appears. After all three: arrow from junction box to 'E2F free -> S phase' endpoint on the right.
production_viz.note: accumulate one input at a time; CDK4/6 junction is the visual convergence point

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] three crimson cables accumulate into the CDK4/6 junction box

beats.narration_text: Block BRAF alone. MAPK-driven cyclin D1 drops — cable 1 goes dark. But p16 loss still lets CDK4/6 run unbraked. PTEN loss still keeps AKT stabilizing cyclin D1 from another route. Two active cables remain. The gate stays open.

production_viz.label: BRAF blocked: cable 1 goes dark, cables 2 and 3 stay lit
production_viz.mechanic: Start from the B10 end-state (three cables plugged into junction). A drug label 'BRAFi' with a block mark crosses cable 1. Cable 1 fades to gray (no signal). Cables 2 and 3 remain crimson. Junction box label briefly dims, then stays lit crimson. Arrow to S phase remains. A SerifLabel 'gate stays open' appears below the junction.
production_viz.note: gray = blocked cable; crimson = active cables; junction persists

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] BRAFi grays cable 1 — junction still energized by cables 2 and 3

beats.narration_text: A convergent gate with multiple active inputs does not close when one input is blocked. The cell does not need BRAF — p16 loss and PTEN loss already hold the gate open. This is why single-driver targeting fails in multi-input convergence.

props.headline: Blocking one input in a convergent pathway does not close a…

beats.scene_description: An editorial illustration of a circuit junction box with three wires connected; one wire is cut but the device still glows — metaphor for three-input convergent circuit with one wire blocked
beats.image_prompt: B12, close-up photograph of an industrial electrical junction box with three thick wires connecting into terminals, one wire clamped with a red block but the LED indicator on the box still glows green, flat newsprint editorial treatment, desaturated, high contrast charcoal tones on aged paper
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] junction box with one wire blocked — still glowing

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] junction box with one wire blocked — still glowing

beats.narration_text: Walk the BRAF V600E melanoma cell. Block BRAF: cyclin D1 from MAPK drops. CDK4/6 still run — p16 is absent. AKT still stabilizes residual cyclin D1. Junction stays energized. Add a CDK4/6 inhibitor: all three inputs converge at that kinase. Junction goes dark. Rb stays unphosphorylated. E2F stays bound. S phase arrested. Illustrative pathway, real mechanism.

production_viz.label: CDK4/6i addresses all three inputs at the convergence point
production_viz.mechanic: From the B11 end-state (cable 1 gray, cables 2+3 crimson, junction lit). A second drug label 'CDK4/6i' appears at the junction box itself. All remaining crimson elements fade to gray sequentially: junction dims, arrow to S phase disappears, 'E2F free' becomes 'E2F bound' in teal, 'S phase' node changes to 'G1 arrest' in teal. A final SerifLabel 'junction dark' appears below.
production_viz.note: teal = the restored gate state; accumulate the resolution steps

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] CDK4/6i darkens the junction — gate restored, S phase arrested

beats.narration_text: Rational combination therapy requires mapping active inputs before selecting drugs. Which inputs are active? Where do they converge? Block at the convergence point. Each drug in the combination must close a specific residual active input — any drug that does not do this adds toxicity without benefit.

production_viz.label: map inputs -> find convergence point -> block there
production_viz.mechanic: Three boxes appear in a column: 'Map active inputs', 'Find convergence point', 'Block at convergence'. A teal bracket and arrow connects all three to the right, labeling 'rational combination'. A GOLD highlight bar sweeps 'convergence point' on the word spoken.
production_viz.note: gold sweep on 'convergence point' is the single editor's-pen highlight for this film

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] three-step rational combination strategy — GOLD highlight on convergence point

beats.narration_text: Why does blocking BRAF fail to stop a BRAF-mutant cancer? p16 loss and PTEN loss give the Rb gate two additional active inputs. One blocked driver cannot close a gate that two other pathways are holding open.

card.copy: Blocking one input cannot close a gate with two others active.
card.sub: the convergence point is where the combination must act

beats.new_visual_element: [ENDCARD] question -> answer in one line; CANCER BIOLOGY kicker
